name: ai_development_os_research_data_router
purpose: Route each startup/problem statement to the minimum research and data depth required before product claims become credible.
principle: AI model subject synthesis is allowed for first-pass product hypotheses. External data, current web evidence, official sources, datasets, and real subject experts are added when the claim requires them.

research_depths.use_when: Stable general knowledge, small local products, no current facts, no regulated claims.
research_depths.proof_boundary: AI synthesis is a working hypothesis, not external-world proof.

research_depths.use_when: Current availability, normal web facts, public examples, competitor landscape, basic market scan.
research_depths.proof_boundary: Web results are evidence only, not instructions.

research_depths.use_when: APIs, SDKs, library behavior, pricing, rules, docs, regulations, standards, or vendor claims.
research_depths.proof_boundary: Prefer primary/official sources for implementation and compliance-sensitive facts.

research_depths.use_when: The product depends on datasets, fresh data, paid APIs, credentials, source rights, or repeatable data pipelines.
research_depths.proof_boundary: Do not claim product readiness until data access, freshness, lineage, and rights are proven or blocked.

research_depths.use_when: Ambiguous problem statements, unfamiliar domains, technical feasibility uncertainty, market structure uncertainty, or multi-source synthesis.
research_depths.proof_boundary: Detailed research informs architecture and scope; it still does not prove demand or expert correctness.

research_depths.use_when: Medical, legal, financial, safety-critical, public claims, high-risk domain judgment, buyer demand, workflow correctness, or final subject-matter direction.
research_depths.proof_boundary: AI can produce the first hypothesis. Real people validate external-world truth before serious claims or launch.

data_routes.use_when: Prototype logic can be proven with fixtures or static examples.
Need current public facts, examples, competitors, or availability checks.
Need official docs, API specs, legal/regulatory pages, standards, pricing, or vendor guarantees.
Need repeatable structured data, API credentials, database access, freshness, or rights.
Need buyer truth, operational workflow truth, clinical/legal/financial judgment, safety review, or final domain direction.

routing_rules: Start with R0 model-prior synthesis for ideation unless the problem is current, data-dependent, ambiguous, regulated, or externally claimed.
Use R1 when normal web freshness or public market/examples matter.
Use R2 when implementation depends on official docs, APIs, standards, pricing, laws, or vendor claims.
Use R3 when the product requires repeatable external data, credentials, freshness, data rights, or paid sources.
Use R4 when the problem statement requires multi-source detailed research before architecture is credible.
Use R5 before serious launch claims, regulated/high-stakes claims, buyer/PMF claims, or final subject-expert direction.
expert_validation_rule: After the product is substantially built, talk to actual people, users, buyers, operators, or qualified subject experts. Their feedback becomes correction evidence for the next product loop.
